item.title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
item.description: A new analysis from OpenAI reveals issues in SWE-Bench Pro, a popular coding benchmark, raising concerns about reliability and accuracy in evaluating AI models.

item.title: Our approach to government and national security partnerships
item.description: Learn how OpenAI approaches government and national security partnerships, with principles for responsible AI use, democratic accountability, and public safety.

item.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

item.title: Flint: A visualization language for the AI era
item.description: Short chart specifications are easy to write, but often produce uninspiring results.

item.title: Jesse Thaler named director of the Laboratory for Nuclear Science
item.description: The professor of physics and inaugural director of the NSF AI Institute for Artificial Intelligence and Fundamental Interactions will lead LNS and continue his research in particle physics.

item.title: Aurora 1.5: Extending open foundation models for weather and Earth-system applications
item.description: Aurora 1.5 adds 22 more variables, hourly temporal resolution, and probabilistic ensemble forecasting to the Aurora foundation model, making it more useful for real-world weather, climate, and energy applications.

item.title: Tiny robot boats build floating structures
item.description: MIT researchers developed FloatForm, a swarm of small aquatic robots that snap together like ants forming a raft, assembling into reconfigurable structures on the water.

item.title: Hugging Face Models on Foundry Managed Compute
Run AI workloads on any cloud, store on Hugging Face: zero-egress storage with SkyPilot
How novice coders can develop AI programs for military applications
item.description: A USAF cadet and a Lincoln Laboratory researcher found AI chatbots can help nontechnical service members produce viable software applications for their unique problems.

item.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue.

item.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue.

item.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
item.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.
